seed_expert_discoveries.technical_takeaway: GPT-5.5 achieves #1 with 70% pass@1 (vs 58% for Claude Opus 4.8) on hard long-horizon coding benchmark, while being ~2x faster, ~1/2 the cost, ~1/3 the output tokens, and 3x more token efficient (47k vs 136k output tokens). Demonstrates superior intelligence per dollar/minute/task.
GPT-5.5 leads in coding benchmarks; GPT 5.6 expected to improve further, widening gap over competitors like Opus 4.8.
Open source AI (Kimi, DeepSeek, GLM) exponentially advancing and suitable for 50% of tasks; token consumption nearing parity with Gemini models.

near_misses.technical_takeaway: Combining AI access rewards with agent infrastructure for utility-focused adoption (bai_agi).
near_misses.reason: Mentions agents but lacks specific technical details, benchmarks, or new frameworks/repos.

notes: Seed experts yielded strong benchmark and efficiency updates on GPT-5.5 and open-source models. Global keyword search returned mostly unrelated/low-signal content; no additional high-signal AI technical innovations (repos, architectures, memory techniques) found in past 24h.
